━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
📚 DAILY MARKET EDUCATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🔍 READING THE COT REPORT LIKE A PRO

The Commitment of Traders report shows you what the big players are actually doing with their money. Released every Friday by the CFTC, it reveals positioning of commercials, large speculators, and retail traders.

Here's what matters:

- Commercials are the smart money. They hedge real business exposure. When they're heavily net long, prices often find support.
- Large speculators are trend followers. Extreme positioning often signals reversals ahead.
- Check the net positions AND the changes week-over-week.

Real example: In March 2026, commercials hit their highest net long position in crude oil in 18 months while speculators were max short. Oil rallied 23 percent over the next 8 weeks.

How to use it:

- Look for extremes in positioning
- Compare current levels to 3-year historical ranges
- Use as confirmation, not a standalone signal
- Best combined with price action and technical levels

PRO TIP: When commercials and speculators are on opposite extremes, the commercials win 70 percent of the time historically. That's your edge.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📊 RSI DIVERGENCES - YOUR EARLY WARNING SYSTEM

RSI divergence happens when price makes a new high or low but the RSI indicator doesn't confirm it. This signals weakening momentum before the trend actually breaks.

Two types you need to know:

Bullish Divergence:
- Price makes lower lows
- RSI makes higher lows
- Signals potential reversal upward
- Best at oversold levels below 30

Bearish Divergence:
- Price makes higher highs
- RSI makes lower highs
- Signals potential reversal downward
- Most reliable at overbought levels above 70

Recent case: EUR/USD in late May 2026 printed higher highs on the daily chart but RSI showed clear lower highs. Three days later, a 280-pip correction began.

Trading the setup:

- Wait for price confirmation, don't jump early
- Use multiple timeframes, daily plus 4H works best
- Combine with support resistance zones
- Set stops beyond the divergence swing point

SMART MONEY RULE: Hidden divergences are even stronger. They occur in the direction of the trend and signal continuation after a pullback. Learn to spot both types.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

💡 3 TIPS FOR TODAY'S MARKET

1. WEEKEND GAP STRATEGY
- Sunday evening openings often gap from Friday close
- Major news over weekend drives these moves
- Wait 2-4 hours for initial volatility to settle
- Trade the gap fill if price extends too far too fast

2. LIQUIDITY AWARENESS
- Sunday and Monday sessions have lower volume
- Spreads widen, slippage increases
- Reduce position sizes by 30-50 percent
- Avoid trading the first 30 minutes of week open

3. MULTI-TIMEFRAME CONFIRMATION
- Before entering any trade, check three timeframes
- Daily for trend, 4H for structure, 1H for entry
- All three should align for highest probability
- Conflicting signals mean stay out or reduce risk

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🛡️ STOP LOSS DISCIPLINE - YOUR SURVIVAL TOOL

The difference between profitable traders and blown accounts is simple: stop loss discipline. No exceptions, no excuses, no moving stops further away when threatened.

Why traders fail at this:

- Emotional attachment to being right
- Hope that price will come back
- Fear of taking small losses
- Moving stops to avoid admitting mistakes

The professional approach:

- Set your stop when you enter the trade, not after
- Place stops at logical technical levels, not arbitrary percentages
- Never move a stop loss further from entry
- If your stop gets hit, the market told you something, listen

Position sizing matters more than you think:

- Risk only 1-2 percent of account per trade
- Your stop distance determines your position size
- Wider stop equals smaller position, not bigger risk
- Calculate before entering: Stop distance x Position size equals Max loss

Real scenario: Trader with 10,000 dollar account risks 100 dollars per trade. Stop is 50 pips away. Position size must be 0.20 lots maximum on EUR/USD. Non-negotiable.

The psychological truth: Taking ten small planned losses is infinitely better than one unplanned catastrophic loss. Your stop loss is your insurance premium.

PRO TIP: If you're consistently getting stopped out, your stops aren't the problem. Your entry timing or market selection is. Fix the root cause, don't blame the stop.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Educational purpose only
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
